name: CRISPR Guide Ranker
description: Ranks a list of CRISPR guide RNA or crRNA candidates by combined on-target efficiency (GC content, no poly-T run, and a Cas9-specific PAM-proximal G bonus when nuclease='cas9') and off-target safety. Specificity is treated as a safety filter: guides get +1 only if they have no HIGH-risk predicted off-targets. MEDIUM-risk and total off-target counts are reported and used as tie-breakers, not scored with arbitrary count cutoffs. Replaces the default 'pick guides[0]' behavior in pipelines with evidence-based guide selection and returns a plain-English rationale for the top pick.

inputs.name: guides
inputs.description: List of guide dicts as returned by design_cas9_grna or design_cas12a_crrna. Each dict must have a 'protospacer' key.

inputs.name: reference
inputs.description: Full DNA sequence to scan for off-targets. Use the 'sequence' field from a crispr_fetch_target_sequence result: pass sequence_info['sequence'], not the gene name (e.g. 'BRCA1'), the resource name, or a truncated summary string (e.g. 'ATGCTGAA... (2139 chars)'). The value must be the complete raw sequence string.

inputs.name: nuclease
inputs.description: Which nuclease the guides target: 'cas9' (default) or 'cas12a'. Affects scoring (Cas9 adds a PAM-proximal G bonus) and PAM recognition in off-target prediction.

inputs.name: max_mismatches
inputs.description: Mismatch threshold passed through to predict_offtargets for the specificity score. Default 3.

outputs.description: A dict with keys: ranked_guides (input guides with added efficiency_score, specificity_score, total_score, and detail dicts; sorted best-first), best_guide (the top-ranked guide), and scoring_rationale (plain-English explanation of why the best guide won).
